policy.title: RBI releases final guidelines on Climate Risk and Sustainable Finance for Scheduled Commercial Banks
policy.description: RBI's final framework mandates climate scenario analysis for banks with assets above ₹1 lakh crore by March 2027, with PD/LGD adjustments for physical risk-exposed portfolios. Banks must report transition risk metrics quarterly starting Q3 FY27.

policy.title: SEBI expands BRSR Core to top 500 listed companies with mandatory assurance
policy.description: SEBI circular extends BRSR Core reporting with reasonable assurance to top 500 companies by market capitalization from FY27, adding Scope 3 emissions and water intensity metrics. Value chain ESG assessment now mandatory for top 250 companies.
policy.source: Securities and Exchange Board of India

policy.title: IRDAI issues master circular on index-based parametric insurance products
policy.description: New framework permits insurers to file parametric products using ISRO, IMD, and private satellite data triggers for weather perils. Basis risk disclosure and 72-hour payout timelines mandated for agriculture and SME segments.
policy.source: Insurance Regulatory and Development Authority of India

policy.title: ISSB releases jurisdictional adoption guide for emerging markets
policy.description: New guidance provides interoperability pathways for IFRS S1/S2 adoption in developing economies, including phased timelines and proportionality provisions for SMEs. India's BRSR explicitly referenced as compatible foundation requiring limited supplementation.

policy.title: Basel Committee finalizes Pillar 3 climate disclosure framework
policy.description: Final standard requires globally systemically important banks to disclose physical and transition risk exposures by asset class from 2028. Standardized metrics include financed emissions intensity and climate VaR for trading book positions.

policy.title: NGFS publishes Phase V climate scenarios with enhanced physical risk pathways
policy.description: Updated scenarios incorporate IPCC AR6 damage functions and regional granularity for South Asia including compound flood-heat events. New 'Hot House World' variant models 3.5°C pathway impacts on agricultural and coastal portfolios.
policy.source: Network for Greening the Financial System

competitors.title: Jupiter Intelligence partners with HDFC Bank for physical risk scoring across commercial real estate portfolio
competitors.description: Multi-year agreement will integrate Jupiter's ClimateScore data into HDFC's credit underwriting for commercial property loans above ₹50 crore. Deal reportedly valued at $4-5 million annually.

competitors.title: Sust Global launches India-specific physical risk API with tehsil-level resolution
competitors.description: New product covers flood, cyclone, drought, and heat stress perils across 5,500+ tehsils with forward projections to 2100. Pricing undercuts US-focused competitors at $0.02 per asset query.

competitors.title: GIST Advisory raises $12 million Series A led by Omnivore Partners
competitors.description: Funding will expand natural capital accounting platform for Indian banks and corporates. Company claims 40+ enterprise clients including three PSU banks using its water risk valuation tools.

competitors.title: Moody's launches Climate Risk Score for Indian NBFC sector
competitors.description: Proprietary scoring covers 200+ NBFCs using satellite-derived flood and heat exposure data overlaid on branch and collateral locations. Product integrates with Moody's Analytics credit models for PD adjustment.

competitors.title: Sprih partners with Tata Capital for automated BRSR Core reporting
competitors.description: Platform will aggregate emissions and social metrics across Tata Capital's lending portfolio of 15,000+ SMEs. Deal positions Sprih as embedded ESG infrastructure for large NBFC segment.

competitors.title: XDI releases open-access physical risk dataset for 2,500 Indian districts
competitors.description: Cross Dependency Initiative publishes free tier of its Gross Domestic Climate Risk data for India covering buildings and infrastructure. Premium API offers asset-level analysis; strategy appears aimed at market penetration.

research.title: Physical Climate Risk in Indian Bank Portfolios: Stress Testing Agriculture and MSME Exposure
research.description: RBI study finds 18% of scheduled commercial bank agricultural loans are in districts facing severe compound drought-heat risk by 2035. Estimates potential NPAs of ₹45,000 crore under RCP 8.5 scenario without adaptation measures.

research.title: Scaling Parametric Insurance in Emerging Markets: Lessons from South Asia
research.description: IFC analysis of 35 parametric pilots finds basis risk remains primary barrier to uptake, with 23% of payouts misaligned with actual losses. Recommends hybrid trigger structures combining satellite indices with ground-truthed loss assessment.

research.title: ESG Data Gaps in Emerging Markets: Implications for Sustainable Finance Flows
research.description: Report quantifies that only 12% of listed companies in South Asia have third-party verified emissions data versus 67% in OECD markets. Data gaps correlate with 40-60 basis point higher cost of sustainable debt issuance.

research.title: Sigma 2/2026: Natural Catastrophes and Climate Risk in Asia-Pacific
research.description: India's insured losses from climate perils reached $8.2 billion in 2025, up 34% from prior year. Report estimates protection gap of $42 billion annually, with flood and cyclone representing 68% of uninsured economic losses.

research.title: Credit Portfolio Alignment: Methodologies for Measuring Financed Emissions in Emerging Markets
research.description: UNEPFI guidance addresses data proxy challenges for Scope 3 financed emissions in markets with limited corporate disclosure. Proposes sector-region emission factors calibrated to India, Brazil, and Southeast Asia contexts.

research.title: Climate Insurance for Resilience: Innovative Products for Agriculture Value Chains
research.description: CPI identifies bundled credit-insurance products as most viable channel for smallholder parametric coverage in India. Analysis shows 3.2x higher uptake when parametric insurance is embedded in Kisan Credit Card disbursement.

ourRead.headline: RBI's final climate guidelines create an 18-month window where Indian banks must procure physical risk analytics — and global players are already positioning to capture this demand.
body: This month's signals converge on a single theme: climate risk quantification for Indian financial institutions is shifting from voluntary exploration to regulatory mandate, and the competitive landscape is intensifying precisely as the opportunity crystallizes. The RBI's final guidelines, SEBI's BRSR expansion, and IRDAI's parametric framework collectively create a compliance-driven demand surge that will peak in late 2026 and early 2027.

The RBI's April 18 guidelines are the most consequential policy development for StepChange in the past year. The explicit requirement for PD/LGD adjustments based on physical risk exposure — with a March 2027 deadline for large banks — transforms our core climate risk intelligence offering from a 'nice-to-have' into procurement-ready infrastructure. The RBI's own research finding that 18% of agricultural loans face severe compound risk provides the empirical anchor banks need to justify budget allocation. We should be citing this study in every client conversation.

The competitor signals demand immediate attention. Jupiter Intelligence's HDFC partnership and Sust Global's India-specific API launch indicate that global players have moved beyond pilot discussions into contracted revenue. XDI's free data tier is a classic market penetration play that could establish them as the default reference dataset. However, Moody's Climate Risk Score for NBFCs is perhaps the most direct competitive threat — they're bundling physical risk with their existing credit analytics relationship, exactly the integration play we've discussed. GIST Advisory's $12 million raise for natural capital accounting also signals investor appetite for India-focused climate data companies, which validates our market but adds a well-funded domestic competitor.

The IFC parametric insurance study and CPI's analysis on bundled credit-insurance products provide the playbook for our parametric bet. The finding that Kisan Credit Card-embedded parametric coverage sees 3.2x higher uptake is actionable intelligence for our bank conversations. We should be positioning our parametric design capabilities around this distribution insight rather than standalone product development. The Swiss Re protection gap estimate of $42 billion annually gives us the market sizing narrative for investor and client conversations.

One thing to watch: whether Indian banks default to global incumbents (Jupiter, Moody's) or build with domestic/emerging market specialists will likely be decided in the next two quarters. One action: we need a direct response to Sust Global's tehsil-level API — either through competitive positioning on accuracy and local calibration, or through partnership conversations before they lock in exclusive bank relationships.
